prompts: Reversible reactions are those where the reactants can be converted to products and vice versa, often achieving an equilibrium where both reactants and products coexist. Irreversible reactions proceed in a single direction, converting reactants entirely to products without the conversion back to reactants. In reversible reactions, the reaction rates of forward and backward paths are significant, while in irreversible reactions, the forward path predominates until completion. Factors like temperature and pressure can shift equilibrium in reversible reactions, whereas irreversible reactions typically complete regardless of external changes once initiated.
